product_verdict.summary: Quaternius candidates improve world/readability for core buildings, but current S0 fallback still has clearer team-color and functional cues. The next stage should combine Quaternius silhouettes with explicit team-color/interaction markers before any runtime import.

category_findings.finding: Quaternius Town Center gives a stronger base-anchor/world read than the boxy S0 proxy, but it lacks obvious player ownership and needs a command-center cue.
Quaternius Barracks reads as a real military building, while S0 is more explicit through shield/swords/team color. Add banner/crest or team-color slot before import.
Quaternius Farm is a clear civilian/supply building and materially improves over the simple S0 farm wedge. Main risk is footprint/scale, not identity.
Quaternius Mine has a better cave/rock body, but S0 still has a stronger gold cue. The likely solution is Quaternius Mine plus Resource Gold accent.
Quaternius Trees look more like a natural resource boundary than the single S0 fallback tree. Needs repeated placement and worker occlusion proof.
Quaternius Watch Tower has strong silhouette, but S0 communicates team ownership better through a color band. Add attack cue and team marker before import.

next_gate: Create team-color/material marker concepts for Town Center, Barracks, Tower, and optionally Farm.
Compose Mine + Resource Gold into a single goldmine preview before runtime import.
Run footprint/selection-ring/worker pathing proof for Town Hall, Barracks, Farm, Goldmine, Tree Line, and Tower.
Keep S0 fallback paths intact and prove GLB load failure returns to fallback.
